channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Apply for the Stanton Fellowship 2026: Support for Los Angeles Change-Makers and Community Leaders
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Stanton Fellowship, offered by the Durfee Foundation, is a prestigious opportunity for experienced leaders in Los Angeles County to step away from day-to-day responsibilities and explore bold, high-impact ideas. Designed to support inquiry, innovation, and cross-sector collaboration, the fellowship provides both funding and time to pursue solutions to complex challenges facing the region. Application […]

The post Apply for the Stanton Fellowship 2026: Support for Los Angeles Change-Makers and Community Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Stanton Fellowship, offered by the Durfee Foundation, is a prestigious opportunity for experienced leaders in Los Angeles County to step away from day-to-day responsibilities and explore bold, high-impact ideas. Designed to support inquiry, innovation, and cross-sector collaboration, the fellowship provides both funding and time to pursue solutions to complex challenges facing the region.

Application Timeline

  • Application Opens: April 1
  • Application Deadline: June 24
  • Site Visits: August 4–27
  • Award Notifications: Early September
  • In-person Orientation: January 2027

About the Stanton Fellowship

The Stanton Fellowship supports up to six leaders each year with $100,000 over two years to investigate a compelling question that has the potential to create meaningful impact in Los Angeles County.

The program is intended for individuals who are deeply embedded in their fields and communities, and who are well-positioned to explore innovative approaches to pressing issues. Fellows are encouraged to pursue adaptive, exploratory work rather than rigid, technical solutions.

Funding is flexible and may be used for:

  • Travel and convenings
  • Research and consulting support
  • Conferences and collaborative activities

The fellowship creates space for leaders to think critically, test ideas, and develop new approaches that may not be feasible within their regular professional roles.

Fellowship Experience

At the core of the program is a collaborative cohort model. Fellows engage with a diverse, cross-disciplinary group of peers, sharing insights and learning from one another’s work and perspectives.

Participation includes:

  • A minimum of three months of dedicated project time over two years (in increments of at least two weeks)
  • Three overnight retreats
  • Six day-long cohort meetings
  • Ongoing knowledge-sharing within professional networks

Fellows are expected to contribute actively to the cohort and disseminate insights from their inquiry to broader audiences.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a resident of Los Angeles County
  • Work at an organization located and operating within Los Angeles County
  • Have at least 10 years of demonstrated leadership and impact in the nonprofit sector, government, arts, social movements, or social entrepreneurship
  • Be in a position to influence peers, policymakers, or the public
  • Present a clearly defined proposal focused on a “big question” to explore over two years
  • Demonstrate financial need for project support (fellowship is supplemental income)
  • Commit to all fellowship activities and time requirements
  • Not currently receive institutional or academic support for similar research
  • Not have received a Durfee Sabbatical within the past five years

The Durfee Foundation strongly encourages applications from organizations serving historically marginalized communities, including BIPOC, LGBTQ, and low-income populations.

Review Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on three core areas:

1. The Candidate

Reviewers assess whether the applicant:

  • Is a recognized thought leader in their field
  • Has a strong reputation and influence related to the proposed topic
  • Demonstrates openness to experimentation and non-linear exploration
  • Has access to platforms or networks to share insights
  • Can commit dedicated time away from daily responsibilities
  • Will actively contribute to and benefit from the fellowship cohort
  • Lacks access to traditional research or inquiry opportunities

2. The Inquiry

The proposed project should:

  • Address a complex and significant issue affecting Los Angeles County
  • Be clearly articulated and thoughtfully planned
  • Demonstrate originality and potential to break new ground
  • Be feasible within the two-year timeframe
  • Include a clear approach to using time and resources effectively

3. Timing

Strong applications will show:

  • The applicant is at the right stage in their career for this opportunity
  • There is urgency or relevance to the proposed inquiry
  • The applicant’s professional and personal circumstances are stable enough to complete the fellowship

Program Goals

The Stanton Fellowship aims to:

  • Enable Los Angeles leaders to pursue in-depth exploration of critical issues
  • Build a diverse, cross-sector community of thinkers and practitioners
  • Generate insights, relationships, and ideas that influence systems and communities across Los Angeles County

How to Apply

Interested candidates should prepare a compelling proposal outlining their inquiry and submit their application before June 24.

APPLY HERE

Discover similar opportunities here. 

The post Apply for the Stanton Fellowship 2026: Support for Los Angeles Change-Makers and Community Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Oxford University Postdoctoral Fellowship in African Studies 2026
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The University of Oxford is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies at the Oxford School of Global and Area Studies (OSGA). This is a prestigious opportunity for early-career scholars to advance their research within a globally recognized academic environment. Overview of the Fellowship Position: Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies Institution: […]

The post Apply Now: Oxford University Postdoctoral Fellowship in African Studies 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Oxford is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies at the Oxford School of Global and Area Studies (OSGA). This is a prestigious opportunity for early-career scholars to advance their research within a globally recognized academic environment.

Overview of the Fellowship

  • Position: Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies
  • Institution: African Studies Centre (ASC), OSGA
  • Location: Oxford, United Kingdom
  • Salary: £39,424 – £47,779 per annum (Grade 7)
  • Contract Type: Full-time (37.5 hours/week), 2-year fixed-term
  • Start Date: 1 October 2026 (or as soon as possible thereafter)
  • Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 (12:00 noon UK time)

About the Fellowship

The African Studies Centre at Oxford is seeking an outstanding early-career researcher to develop an innovative and independent research programme under the broad theme of “Africa and the World.” The fellowship focuses on understanding Africa’s place within contemporary global, comparative, and transnational contexts.

Working under the supervision of Rita Abrahamsen, the successful candidate will contribute to advancing cutting-edge scholarship and strengthening Oxford’s position as a leading hub for African Studies.

Key Responsibilities

The selected Fellow will:

  • Conduct an independent and ambitious research programme in African Studies
  • Publish high-quality research in peer-reviewed journals and academic presses
  • Present research at conferences, seminars, and academic forums
  • Contribute to shaping the “Africa and the World” research agenda at Oxford
  • Organize seminars, conferences, and collaborative research initiatives
  • Teach an option course aligned with their expertise
  • Supervise master’s dissertations in the MSc in African Studies
  • Actively participate in the academic community at OSGA and ASC

Benefits of the Position

Working at the University of Oxford offers a comprehensive benefits package, including:

  • 38 days of annual leave (inclusive of public holidays), with options to extend
  • Generous family leave schemes, including maternity, adoption, and paternity leave
  • A competitive pension scheme
  • Sustainable commuting options, including cycle loans and discounted travel
  • Access to a vibrant community of social, cultural, and sports clubs

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should:

  • Hold (or be close to completing) a relevant doctoral degree
  • Have completed their doctorate no earlier than three years before 1 January 2026
  • Demonstrate strong academic writing and publication potential
  • Show experience presenting research at conferences and seminars
  • Have a commitment to teaching and postgraduate supervision

Desirable Experience

  • Teaching and supervising theses
  • Leading independent research projects
  • Collaborating with other researchers

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The Oxford School of Global and Area Studies is committed to fostering an inclusive academic environment. Applications are particularly encouraged from women and individuals from Black and minority ethnic backgrounds, who are underrepresented in academic roles at Oxford.

The School also holds a Bronze Athena Swan Award, recognizing its commitment to advancing gender equality.

Application Process

Candidates must apply through the official Oxford University recruitment portal. The application requires:

  • A Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • A supporting statement addressing the selection criteria
  • A research proposal outlining the proposed project

Applicants are advised not to submit published work at this stage.

Read more and Apply Here

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 (12:00 noon UK time)
  • Interview Date: 14 May 2026 (online)

This fellowship presents a unique opportunity to join a globally respected institution and contribute to influential research on Africa’s role in the world. Scholars with strong, original research ideas and a commitment to academic excellence are highly encouraged to apply.

Discover more similar opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: Oxford University Postdoctoral Fellowship in African Studies 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NSF TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs — Up to $1M/Year to Expand AI Skills and Access
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF), in partnership with the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA NIFA), the United States Department of Labor, and the Small Business Administration, is inviting applications for the TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs initiative. This major funding opportunity supports the creation of state- and territory-based hubs designed to expand AI […]

The post NSF TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs — Up to $1M/Year to Expand AI Skills and Access appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF), in partnership with the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA NIFA), the United States Department of Labor, and the Small Business Administration, is inviting applications for the TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs initiative.

This major funding opportunity supports the creation of state- and territory-based hubs designed to expand AI access, literacy, and workforce readiness across the United States.

About the Initiative

The TechAccess program aims to strengthen the U.S. workforce and economy by ensuring that individuals, businesses, and communities are equipped to understand and adopt artificial intelligence technologies.

Selected Coordination Hubs will serve as regional centers for AI education, training, and adoption support, helping bridge the gap between emerging technologies and real-world application.

What the Hubs Will Do

Funded hubs will:

Provide AI literacy and foundational education programs
Deliver applied AI skills training for workers and businesses
Offer technical assistance to support AI adoption
Facilitate hands-on learning opportunities, including internships and project-based experiences
Strengthen regional innovation ecosystems and workforce development

Funding Details

Award amount: Up to $1,000,000 per year per hub
Duration: 3 years (with potential for a 4th year)
Funding supports large-scale, collaborative, and impact-driven initiatives

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is targeted at organizations capable of leading regional initiatives, including:

Universities and research institutions
Nonprofits and workforce development organizations
Public-private partnerships
State or territory-based entities

Applicants should demonstrate the capacity to coordinate multi-stakeholder efforts and deliver measurable impact in AI readiness.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As AI continues to reshape industries, access to skills and tools becomes critical. This initiative ensures that workers, small businesses, and underserved communities are not left behind in the digital transformation.

It is a strategic investment in building an inclusive, AI-ready workforce and economy.

Application Deadline

📅 June 23, 2026 (5:00 PM EDT)

Informational Webinar

📅 Date: April 14, 2026
⏰ Time: 1:00 PM EDT


🔗 Register here.

For more Grant Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post NSF TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs — Up to $1M/Year to Expand AI Skills and Access appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI Fellows Program 2026 at Gates Foundation India: Apply to Work on High-Impact AI Solutions
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation India office is now accepting applications for its first-ever AI Fellows cohort—a unique opportunity to apply artificial intelligence to real-world challenges in health, agriculture, and development. This 12-month fellowship places selected candidates on high-impact projects, where they will work closely with program teams and receive structured mentorship while building […]

The post AI Fellows Program 2026 at Gates Foundation India: Apply to Work on High-Impact AI Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation India office is now accepting applications for its first-ever AI Fellows cohort—a unique opportunity to apply artificial intelligence to real-world challenges in health, agriculture, and development.

This 12-month fellowship places selected candidates on high-impact projects, where they will work closely with program teams and receive structured mentorship while building innovative AI-driven solutions.

About the Program

Each fellow will be embedded within Strategic Initiatives or Programmatic Strategy Teams, contributing to priority projects that address critical development challenges. Fellows will also be supported by experienced mentors and gain exposure to interdisciplinary collaboration across sectors.

Fellowship Focus Areas

Five fellows will be selected, each working on a specialized AI application area:

AI Fellow #1: Maternal & Fetal Health Risk Stratification

Work with longitudinal pregnancy datasets to improve maternal and fetal health outcomes.
Develop AI models for early detection of conditions such as gestational diabetes and preeclampsia.
Build prototype risk-scoring tools for frontline healthcare decision-making.

AI Fellow #2: AI Agricultural Advisory

Integrate weather, satellite, and agronomic data into advisory tools for farmers.
Develop conversational AI interfaces (voice and text) for smallholder engagement.
Support climate risk prediction and crop advisory systems.

AI Fellow #3: AI for Immunization Coverage

Apply AI/ML to identify underserved populations (zero-dose and dropout children).
Develop targeting algorithms and dashboards to improve vaccination outreach.
Enhance last-mile delivery and monitoring systems.

AI Fellow #4: AI for Drug Discovery

Explore AI applications in target identification, molecule design, and screening.
Work with multimodal datasets including genomics and clinical data.
Collaborate with research institutions to strengthen AI-driven discovery pipelines.

AI Fellow #5: Rapid Prototyping of Applied AI Solutions

Develop AI prototypes across sectors like health, education, and agriculture.
Use tools such as chatbots, knowledge graphs, and AI frameworks.
Promote responsible AI practices and build internal toolkits and learning resources.

Benefits

Hands-on experience working on real-world AI projects
Mentorship from experts within the foundation
Exposure to global development challenges and interdisciplinary teams
Opportunity to build scalable, impactful AI solutions
Access to a strong professional network

Why This Opportunity Matters

This fellowship goes beyond theory—offering a chance to directly contribute to solutions that improve health systems, food security, and economic opportunities. It is ideal for individuals who want to use AI for meaningful, large-scale impact.

How to Apply

🔗 Apply here

For more Fellowship opportunities, visit OFY here.

The post AI Fellows Program 2026 at Gates Foundation India: Apply to Work on High-Impact AI Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Proposals: ACE Microgrants 2026 for African Climate and Environmental Initiatives
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Pulitzer Center is now inviting applications for the Africa Climate and Environment (ACE) Microgrants 2026, an exciting opportunity designed to support African local movements working at the intersection of climate, environment, and governance. About the ACE Microgrants The ACE Microgrants are part of the Pulitzer Center’s Africa outreach program, aimed at funding innovative engagement […]

The post Call for Proposals: ACE Microgrants 2026 for African Climate and Environmental Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pulitzer Center is now inviting applications for the Africa Climate and Environment (ACE) Microgrants 2026, an exciting opportunity designed to support African local movements working at the intersection of climate, environment, and governance.

About the ACE Microgrants

The ACE Microgrants are part of the Pulitzer Center’s Africa outreach program, aimed at funding innovative engagement projects that creatively incorporate stories supported by the Center. These projects are expected to drive meaningful conversations and action around environmental transparency, accountability, and governance.

By leveraging storytelling and community engagement, the initiative seeks to deepen public understanding of critical environmental and climate challenges across Africa.

What Kind of Projects Are Supported?

Applicants are encouraged to submit creative and impactful outreach projects that engage:

  • Young professionals
  • Local communities
  • Stakeholders across sectors

Projects should focus on key issues such as:

  • Government management of natural resources
  • Harmful industrial and corporate practices
  • Conflicts of interest and corruption
  • Impacts on biodiversity and ecosystems
  • Indigenous communities and human rights
  • Climate change and environmental sustainability

Successful proposals will demonstrate the ability to:

  • Amplify community voices and lived experiences
  • Encourage dialogue among media, government, civil society, and businesses
  • Inspire action through storytelling and public engagement

Why You Should Apply

This is a unique opportunity to:

  • Secure funding for your climate or environmental initiative
  • Engage communities in meaningful dialogue and advocacy
  • Collaborate across sectors to influence policy and governance
  • Contribute to transparency and accountability in Africa’s environmental landscape

If you’re part of a local movement passionate about climate action and governance, the ACE Microgrants could be your chance to turn ideas into impactful change.

Eligible Applicants

The program is open to a wide range of African local movements, including:

  • Civil society organizations
  • Media organizations
  • Social enterprises
  • Think tanks
  • Community-based associations

Types of Activities

The ACE Microgrants support diverse outreach formats, such as:

  • Conferences and workshops
  • Exhibitions and storytelling events
  • Awareness campaigns
  • Community dialogues and forums

Projects should be inspired by Pulitzer Center-supported reporting on environmental governance and transparency.

Language Requirements

Applications can be submitted in:

  • English
  • French

Application Deadline

April 30, 2026

APPLY HERE

Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official guidelines for detailed eligibility criteria and the selection process before applying.

Discover more similar opportunities here. 

The post Call for Proposals: ACE Microgrants 2026 for African Climate and Environmental Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Conservation Lead Role at the Crop Trust: Drive Global Action on Food Plant Biodiversity
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Crop Trust is hiring a Global Conservation Lead to play a key role in advancing global efforts to protect and conserve food plant biodiversity. This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about sustainability, agriculture, and global conservation to contribute to a high-impact international initiative. About the Role The Global Conservation Lead will coordinate […]

The post Global Conservation Lead Role at the Crop Trust: Drive Global Action on Food Plant Biodiversity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Crop Trust is hiring a Global Conservation Lead to play a key role in advancing global efforts to protect and conserve food plant biodiversity.

This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about sustainability, agriculture, and global conservation to contribute to a high-impact international initiative.

About the Role

The Global Conservation Lead will coordinate engagement within the Global Conservation Consortium for Food Plants (GCCFP)—a major global initiative focused on safeguarding plant diversity critical for food security.

This role involves working with international partners, coordinating conservation strategies, and helping drive forward collaborative efforts to protect vital plant species.

Key Responsibilities

Lead coordination of activities within the GCCFP
Engage with global stakeholders and partners
Support strategic planning and implementation of conservation initiatives
Drive collaboration across institutions and regions
Contribute to advancing global food security and biodiversity goals

Who Should Apply

This role is ideal for professionals with:

Experience in conservation, agriculture, or environmental science
Strong project management and coordination skills
Interest in global sustainability and biodiversity
Ability to work with international teams and stakeholders

Why This Opportunity Matters

Food plant diversity is essential for global food systems, climate resilience, and sustainable development. This role offers a chance to contribute directly to preserving biodiversity and ensuring food security for future generations.

How to Apply

🔗 Apply here.

For more Job roles, Visit OFY here.

The post Global Conservation Lead Role at the Crop Trust: Drive Global Action on Food Plant Biodiversity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Tax Fairness Fellowship 2026: Paid Opportunity for Professionals Shaping Fairer Tax Systems
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Tax Fairness Fellowship is now open for applications, offering a paid, career-transition opportunity for professionals ready to shape fairer and more equitable tax systems. This unique fellowship is designed for experienced individuals who want to pivot into impactful work around tax justice, economic fairness, and public policy reform. About the Fellowship The Tax Fairness […]

The post Tax Fairness Fellowship 2026: Paid Opportunity for Professionals Shaping Fairer Tax Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Tax Fairness Fellowship is now open for applications, offering a paid, career-transition opportunity for professionals ready to shape fairer and more equitable tax systems.

This unique fellowship is designed for experienced individuals who want to pivot into impactful work around tax justice, economic fairness, and public policy reform.

About the Fellowship

The Tax Fairness Fellowship brings together a diverse cohort of professionals from different sectors to build expertise and contribute to fairer tax systems globally.

It is especially suited for individuals looking to transition into the tax justice space, regardless of whether their previous experience directly relates to taxation.

The programme values transferable skills, creativity, and real-world impact over traditional career paths.

Who Should Apply

This fellowship is ideal for professionals with 5+ years of experience in areas such as:

Tax law
Finance or economics
Public policy
Advocacy and campaigns
Communications and storytelling
Community organizing
Movement or coalition building
Fundraising

Applicants with non-linear career paths or career changers are strongly encouraged to apply.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

Have at least 5 years of professional experience
Demonstrate strong problem-solving and leadership abilities
Be mission-driven and committed to social impact
Be authorized to work in one of the following regions:

Canada
European Union
Kenya
United States

Benefits

Paid fellowship opportunity
Career transition support into the tax justice field
Exposure to global economic and policy issues
Opportunity to work on meaningful, real-world challenges
Access to a network of experts, advocates, and changemakers

Why This Opportunity Matters

Tax systems play a critical role in shaping inequality and economic opportunity. This fellowship empowers professionals to influence policy, advocate for fairness, and contribute to systemic change.

If you’re looking to shift your career toward meaningful impact, this is a powerful entry point into the world of tax justice.

Application Deadline

📅 April 27, 2026

How to Apply

🔗 Apply here

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Tax Fairness Fellowship 2026: Paid Opportunity for Professionals Shaping Fairer Tax Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Attend Oxford Africa Conference 2026: Key Speakers, Agenda and How to Get Tickets
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Oxford Africa Conference 2026 returns for its 16th edition, bringing together some of the most influential voices shaping Africa’s future. Hosted at the prestigious University of Oxford by the Oxford University Africa Society, this year’s conference promises two days of powerful dialogue, networking, and action-driven insights. Event Details Venue: Mathematical Institute, University of Oxford […]

The post Attend Oxford Africa Conference 2026: Key Speakers, Agenda and How to Get Tickets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Oxford Africa Conference 2026 returns for its 16th edition, bringing together some of the most influential voices shaping Africa’s future. Hosted at the prestigious University of Oxford by the Oxford University Africa Society, this year’s conference promises two days of powerful dialogue, networking, and action-driven insights.

Event Details

  • Venue: Mathematical Institute, University of Oxford (Andrew Wiles Building)
  • Dates: Saturday, 16 May 2026 – Sunday, 17 May 2026
  • Theme: Anchoring Africa: Grounded Leadership in the Age of Disruption
  • Tickets: Available now — secure your spot early as tickets sell out fast

Welcome to Oxford Africa Conference 2026

The Oxford Africa Conference continues to serve as a leading platform for thought leadership on Africa’s most pressing issues. This year’s theme reflects a critical moment in Africa’s journey—marked by rapid shifts in politics, economics, technology, and climate.

Over two impactful days in Oxford, the conference will convene leaders across policy, business, finance, academia, and culture to:

  • Share diverse perspectives
  • Challenge prevailing assumptions
  • Foster meaningful collaborations

The goal is simple yet powerful: ensure conversations translate into long-term impact across Africa and the global African community.

 Conference Impact

Beyond dialogue, the conference is committed to turning ideas into action. By spotlighting bold innovations and supporting transformative initiatives, the Oxford Africa Conference continues to catalyze change across the continent.

 Keynote Speaker

One of the headline speakers for 2026 is:

  • Julius Maada Bio – President of the Republic of Sierra Leone

Expect insights from top-level leadership on governance, development, and Africa’s evolving global role.

Featured Panels

The conference features a diverse lineup of panels addressing Africa’s most critical sectors:

  • Grounded Governance – Public trust, institutional reform, and state capacity
  • Engines of Growth – Economic transformation and investment opportunities
  • Founders in Focus – Entrepreneurship and innovation ecosystems
  • Narrating Africa – Storytelling, media, and cultural influence
  • Powering Africa’s Energy Futures – Sustainable energy solutions
  • Building Africa’s Digital Capital – Technology and digital transformation

Spotlight Panel: Grounded Governance

This session explores how African states can rebuild trust, strengthen accountability, and deliver services effectively at scale.

 Fireside Chats & Networking

Engage in intimate, unscripted conversations with top leaders during fireside chats designed to go beyond surface-level discussions.

Social Experiences

  • Gala Dinner: A unique opportunity to dine and connect with speakers and delegates in a historic Oxford college setting
  • AfroBop After Party: Celebrate African culture, music, and community in a vibrant closing event

Featured Speakers

Meet a dynamic lineup of innovators and changemakers, including:

  • Theo Baloyi – Founder & Group CEO, Bathu
  • Peace Hyde – Creator & Executive Producer of Young, Famous & African
  • Ron Chari – Policy and governance leader
  • Rose M. Mutiso – Founder, African Tech Futures Lab

More speakers and moderators will be announced soon.

 Accommodation Options

Attendees can choose from a range of convenient and comfortable accommodations near the venue, including:

  • Rhodes Trust
  • Vanbrugh House Hotel
  • Courtyard by Marriott Oxford
  • Voco Oxford Spires

Additional options include The Head of the River and Bear and Ragged Staff.

Travel Information

The conference has partnered with Kenya Airways as the official travel partner.

  • Discount: 15% off flights
  • Promo Code: OAC26KE
  • Booking Window: Now until 17 May 2026
  • Travel Dates: 9 May – 24 May 2026

Delegates are encouraged to book early to take advantage of this exclusive offer.

Secure Your Spot

Be part of a transformative experience shaping Africa’s future. With limited seats available and high demand expected, early registration is strongly recommended.

Get your tickets now and join the conversation driving Africa forward.

Discover more similar opportunities Here.

The post Attend Oxford Africa Conference 2026: Key Speakers, Agenda and How to Get Tickets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: TWAS Fellowships & Research Opportunities 2026: Fully Funded PhD, Postdoctoral Visits to Germany, and Global Research Collaboration Programs
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS), in partnership with leading global institutions such as the German Research Foundation (DFG) and the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) India, is offering a range of fully funded fellowship and research opportunities for 2026. These programmes are designed to support scientists from developing countries through advanced research […]

The post TWAS Fellowships & Research Opportunities 2026: Fully Funded PhD, Postdoctoral Visits to Germany, and Global Research Collaboration Programs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS), in partnership with leading global institutions such as the German Research Foundation (DFG) and the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) India, is offering a range of fully funded fellowship and research opportunities for 2026. These programmes are designed to support scientists from developing countries through advanced research training, international collaboration, and capacity building.

This comprehensive guide covers three major TWAS opportunities:

  1. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (Sub-Saharan Africa)
  2. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (MENA Region)
  3. TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme (PhD in India)

Each programme provides unique benefits, from short-term research visits in Germany to fully funded doctoral studies in India.

1. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (Sub-Saharan Africa)

The TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme for Sub-Saharan Africa offers postdoctoral researchers the opportunity to undertake a fully funded three-month research visit to Germany.

Programme Overview

  • Duration: 3 months
  • Host Country: Germany
  • Target Group: Postdoctoral researchers in Sub-Saharan Africa
  • Deadline: April 15, 2026

The programme is designed to initiate and strengthen research collaborations between African scientists and German institutions, with the goal of building long-term academic partnerships.

Key Benefits

Participants will receive:

  • Full travel expenses to Germany
  • Subsistence allowance during the stay
  • Access to advanced research facilities
  • Opportunity to establish international research collaborations

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD obtained in 2021 or later
    • Special extension for women: up to 6 additional years depending on number of children
  • Be currently employed at a research institution in Sub-Saharan Africa
  • Have at least 5 years of experience in the science system
  • Not already be in Germany at the time of application
  • Not have an established collaboration with the German host

Required Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • Passport copy
  • Invitation letter from a German host institution
  • Two reference letters
  • PhD certificate
  • Proof of language proficiency (English or German)
  • Institutional support letter

Important Notes

  • Visits must take place within 12 months of award
  • No family support is provided
  • Only one TWAS application is allowed per year

Learn more and apply here

2. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (MENA Region)

This programme mirrors the Sub-Saharan Africa version but is tailored for researchers in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

Programme Overview

  • Duration: 3 months
  • Host Country: Germany
  • Target Group: Postdoctoral researchers in MENA
  • Deadline: April 15, 2026

The goal is to foster collaboration between MENA researchers and German scientists.

Key Benefits

  • Travel funding
  • Living expenses covered
  • Access to German research institutions
  • Opportunity for long-term collaboration

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD (2021 or later, with extensions for women)
  • Be employed in a MENA-based institution
  • Have at least 5 years of research or academic experience
  • Not have prior collaboration with the host institution

Application Requirements

Similar to the SSA programme, including:

  • Invitation letter
  • References
  • Academic credentials
  • Language proof
  • Institutional support

Additional Notes

  • Women scientists are strongly encouraged to apply
  • All academic disciplines are eligible
  • Collaboration must be newly initiated

Learn more and apply here

3. TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme (PhD in India)

The TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme offers fully funded PhD opportunities for students from developing countries to study in India.

Programme Overview

  • Level: PhD
  • Duration: Up to 4 years
  • Host Country: India
  • Deadline: May 6, 2026

This programme supports research in cutting-edge areas of science and technology.

Fields of Study

Eligible disciplines include:

  • Agricultural Sciences
  • Biology and Biotechnology
  • Medical and Health Sciences
  • Chemistry
  • Engineering
  • Physics
  • Mathematics
  • Astronomy and Earth Sciences

Fellowship Types

  1. Full-Time Fellowship
    • For applicants not enrolled in a PhD
    • Duration: Up to 4 years
  2. Sandwich Fellowship
    • For PhD students already enrolled in their home country
    • Duration: 6 months to 3 years

Benefits

  • Monthly stipend
  • Subsidized accommodation
  • Health insurance
  • Access to CSIR research facilities

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be under 35 years old
  • Be nationals of developing countries (excluding India)
  • Hold a Master’s degree in a relevant field
  • Be employed in their home country
  • Provide proof of English proficiency
  • Secure acceptance from a CSIR institution

Required Documents

  • Acceptance letter from CSIR institution
  • Academic certificates
  • Research proposal
  • Proof of employment
  • English proficiency

Important Notes

  • Applicants must return to their home country after completion
  • No concurrent assignments allowed
  • Only one TWAS programme application per year

Learn more and apply here

Why You Should Apply

These TWAS opportunities offer:

  • Fully funded international research experience
  • Access to world-class institutions
  • Career advancement in science and academia
  • Global collaboration opportunities
  • Exposure to advanced research environments

Application Deadlines Summary

  • TWAS-DFG (SSA): April 15, 2026
  • TWAS-DFG (MENA): April 15, 2026
  • TWAS-CSIR PhD: May 6, 2026

Discover more global Fellowship and Scholarship opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY).

The post TWAS Fellowships & Research Opportunities 2026: Fully Funded PhD, Postdoctoral Visits to Germany, and Global Research Collaboration Programs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are a young climate advocate, environmental innovator, or aspiring global change-maker, the 2026 Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub Youth Event offers one of the most impactful international platforms to elevate your voice, build your skills, and collaborate with global peers. Organized under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), this […]

The post Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a young climate advocate, environmental innovator, or aspiring global change-maker, the 2026 Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub Youth Event offers one of the most impactful international platforms to elevate your voice, build your skills, and collaborate with global peers. Organized under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), this prestigious event is designed to empower youth to lead meaningful climate action at all levels.

What Is the ACE Hub Youth Event?

The ACE Hub Youth Event is a flagship international gathering under the broader Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub, a global initiative focused on equipping individuals—especially youth—with the knowledge, skills, and tools to actively participate in climate action.

The ACE Hub itself is a collaboration between UN Climate Change and the Government of North Rhine-Westphalia in Germany, supporting the implementation of climate education, public awareness, training, and participation under the Paris Agreement.

This annual youth event is specifically designed to:

  • Build the capacity of young leaders to drive climate solutions
  • Promote collaboration and innovation in climate initiatives
  • Facilitate global networking and knowledge exchange
  • Strengthen youth engagement in policy and grassroots action

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the ACE Hub Youth Event is not just about attending a conference—it is about becoming part of a global movement shaping climate policy and action.

Key Benefits

  • Gain hands-on training in climate advocacy and project design
  • Engage with international experts and policymakers
  • Collaborate with youth leaders from across the world
  • Strengthen your leadership and communication skills
  • Expand your global network in climate and sustainability
  • Opportunity to attend sessions linked to UN climate negotiations

Participants also benefit from immersive learning experiences including workshops, peer-led sessions, and expert-led discussions.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

This opportunity targets young climate leaders and changemakers who are actively engaged in climate-related initiatives.

Eligible Category of People

  • Youth leaders
  • Climate activists and advocates
  • Environmental professionals
  • Students and early-career researchers
  • Community organizers working on climate solutions

General Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be aged 18 to 35 years
  • Have proficiency in English (spoken and written)
  • Be able to attend the full event in person in Bonn, Germany
  • Demonstrate interest or experience in climate action
  • Be engaged in areas such as:
    • Climate education
    • Public awareness
    • Community-based climate initiatives
    • Policy advocacy

Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to all countries worldwide
  • Additional slots available for local youth from North Rhine-Westphalia (Germany)

Program Structure and Activities

The ACE Hub Youth Event is structured as a 3-day intensive experience combining learning, collaboration, and innovation.

What to Expect

  • Day 1: Skills-building workshops and peer-led sessions
  • Day 2: Collaborative group work on climate solutions
  • Day 3: Networking, presentations, and knowledge exchange

Participants will:

  • Present their own climate initiatives
  • Work on solving real-world environmental challenges
  • Participate in expert-led sessions on communication and advocacy

The program emphasizes interactive learning and practical application, ensuring participants leave with actionable skills.

Themes and Focus Areas

The 2026 edition continues to focus on youth leadership and climate entrepreneurship, with strong emphasis on:

  • Community-based climate action
  • Innovation and sustainable solutions
  • Climate communication and advocacy
  • Local-to-global climate impact strategies

Funding and Financial Support

While the event includes both funded and self-funded participation options:

  • A limited number of international participants may receive funding support
  • Additional participants may attend through self-funding or independent accreditation

Funding (when available) may cover:

  • Travel expenses
  • Accommodation
  • Participation costs

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate change is one of the most urgent global challenges of our time, and youth are at the forefront of driving solutions. The ACE Hub Youth Event directly contributes to empowering young people under Article 6 of the UNFCCC and Article 12 of the Paris Agreement, which focus on education, awareness, and participation.

By participating, you are not only gaining skills—you are becoming part of a global ecosystem working toward sustainable and equitable climate solutions.

How to Apply

To apply for the ACE Hub Youth Event 2026:

  1. Complete the official online application form
  2. Provide details of your climate-related experience
  3. Demonstrate your motivation and impact potential
  4. Submit before the deadline

Apply here

Deadline

10th April 2026

The ACE Hub Youth Event 2026 in Bonn, Germany is more than just a conference—it is a transformative experience for young individuals committed to climate action. Whether you are already leading initiatives or just starting your journey, this opportunity provides the platform, resources, and network needed to amplify your impact.

For more information click here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
